metadata.description: Prioritized dataset backfill queue for autism genomics atlas. Organized by integration phase (1-critical, 2-high, 3-medium, 4-low) based on significance to autism research and atlas completion goals

phase_1_critical.description: Foundational primary sequencing and reference datasets essential for core atlas functionality

entries.name: SPARK: Simons Foundation Autism Research Initiative
entries.notes: Largest autism sequencing cohort, essential for prevalence and burden analyses. De-identified whole exome sequencing with extensive phenotype data including autism severity.

entries.name: MSSNG (Missing Sequences)
entries.notes: Whole genome sequencing with structural variant calling. Critical for noncoding variant analysis and regulatory element discovery. Brain Institute Canada resource.

entries.name: Simons Simplex Collection
entries.notes: High-quality family-based data with both parents and siblings sequenced. Essential for de novo variant detection and inheritance pattern analysis.

entries.name: Autism Speaks MSSNG Powered by Amazon
entries.notes: Public genome cohort from Autism Speaks. Includes diverse populations and comprehensive phenotyping including comorbid epilepsy status.

entries.name: Epi25 Collaborative
entries.notes: Large-scale epilepsy sequencing consortium. Essential for understanding epilepsy-autism comorbidity through receptor separation framework.

entries.name: ENCODE Project
entries.notes: Comprehensive functional genomics including ChIP-seq, ATAC-seq, DNase-seq for regulatory annotation. Critical for noncoding variant interpretation.

entries.name: GTEx: Genotype-Tissue Expression
entries.notes: Multi-tissue gene expression with eQTL mapping. Essential for variant effect prediction and understanding tissue-specific regulatory elements.

entries.name: BrainSpan: Atlas of the Developing Human Brain
entries.notes: Developmental expression across prenatal and postnatal periods. Critical for understanding when and where autism-relevant genes are expressed.

entries.name: PsychENCODE Consortium
entries.notes: Single-cell transcriptomics of developing and adult brain. Essential for cell-type specific expression of autism-relevant genes and receptor types.

entries.name: Allen Brain Atlas - Cell Type Taxonomy
entries.notes: Comprehensive single-cell and spatial transcriptomics of mouse and human brain. Critical for understanding cell-type specificity of receptor types and autism genes.

phase_2_high.description: Published studies with functional insights, effect sizes, and mechanistic data relevant to autism genomics

entries.name: Zhou et al. 2019 - De novo regulatory mutations
entries.notes: Seminal paper on de novo mutations in noncoding regions of autism cases. Demonstrates enrichment in regulatory elements.

entries.name: An et al. 2018 - Autism whole genome sequencing
entries.notes: Large-scale WGS of autism families. Important for understanding burden of rare variants genome-wide including noncoding regions.

entries.name: Werling et al. 2018 - De novo structural variants
entries.notes: Comprehensive structural variant discovery in autism. Essential for understanding CNV and SV burden, including regulatory element disruptions.

entries.name: Turner et al. 2017 - Recurrent de novo variants
entries.notes: Identification of recurrent de novo mutations in autism. Key for gene discovery and effect size estimation.

entries.name: Pugsley et al. 2024 - Noncoding variant association
entries.notes: Recent large-scale analysis of noncoding variant associations in autism. Critical for regulatory variant interpretation.

entries.name: Cell Genomics 2024 - Autism gene regulatory networks
entries.notes: 2024 study linking autism variants to gene regulatory networks. Essential for understanding noncoding variant mechanisms.

entries.name: Cell Genomics 2024 - GABA neuron development
entries.notes: Recent single-cell study of GABAergic neuron development relevant to receptor separation model.

phase_3_medium.description: Systematic datasets from public repositories covering autism and neuropsychiatric conditions

entries.name: GEO Autism Gene Expression Studies
entries.notes: Systematic collection of autism and ASD-related gene expression studies from GEO database. ~15-20 relevant studies identified.

entries.name: BioLINK: Brain expression in ASD
entries.notes: Brain expression linked to intellectual disability and autism candidate genes.

entries.name: Human Protein Atlas - Brain
entries.notes: Comprehensive protein expression data including brain tissues. Useful for validating gene expression patterns.

entries.name: SynaptomeDB - Synaptic Genes
entries.notes: Curated database of synaptic genes with functional annotations. Relevant for understanding receptor and ion channel interactions.

entries.name: DisGeNET - Autism-Gene Associations
entries.notes: Comprehensive disease-gene association database. Mining for autism and comorbid condition associations.

entries.name: OMIM - Autism Gene Curation
entries.notes: Manual curation of autism-associated genes with clinical phenotypes and inheritance patterns.

phase_4_low.description: Adjacent conditions and comparative datasets providing mechanistic context

entries.name: Schizophrenia Commonalities Study
entries.notes: Schizophrenia sequencing cohort for understanding shared genetic architecture with autism in GABAergic pathways.

entries.name: ADHD Large-Scale Genetics Consortium
entries.notes: GWAS and sequencing data from ADHD cohorts. Relevant for understanding convergent neurotransmitter pathways.

entries.name: Intellectual Disability Brain Transcriptomics
entries.notes: Gene expression studies in ID cohorts overlapping with autism. Important for understanding shared mechanisms.

entries.name: Mouse Autism Models - Brain Transcriptomics
entries.notes: Single-cell data from mouse models of autism carrying human disease variants. Translational value for mechanistic understanding.

entries.name: Developmental Delay Primary Care Study
entries.notes: Primary care developmental delay cohort. Provides context for autism as part of broader neurodevelopmental spectrum.

entries.name: Sensory Processing in ASD - fMRI Dataset
entries.notes: Neuroimaging data from autism cohorts. Provides phenotypic context for genetic findings.

integration_status_by_phase.phase_1: All datasets required for atlas foundation. Expected completion within 6 months of initiation.
integration_status_by_phase.phase_2: High-impact published studies. Support fine-grained association analysis and mechanistic validation. Expected completion within 3-6 months after phase 1.
integration_status_by_phase.phase_3: Systematic collection of public resources. Lower priority but provides breadth of coverage. Can proceed in parallel with phases 1-2.
integration_status_by_phase.phase_4: Comparative and contextual datasets. Lowest priority but add mechanistic depth. Integration based on available resources.
